machinery_genes.quote: we established a heterologous ExeD assembly system in and showed that ExeAB and ExeC are the only proteins required for the assembly of the ExeD secretin
machinery_genes.note: ExeC named as a component of the A. hydrophila exe T2SS; required together with ExeAB for ExeD secretin assembly. exeC is the first gene of the exeC-N operon (= gspC-N per Ast et al 2002, PMID 11967081). Family GspC from the apparatus brief letter-for-letter exe=gsp mapping. Apparatus paper is on A. hydrophila (same Exe system family); A. salmonicida A449 carries the orthologous operon.

machinery_genes.quote: Expression of the ExeAB complex of Aeromonas hydrophila is required for the localization and assembly of the ExeD secretion port multimer
machinery_genes.note: ExeD is the outer-membrane secretin of the A. hydrophila Exe T2SS; assembles into a multimeric secretion port (dodecameric secretin, Vanderlinde 2017 PMID 28138102). Second gene of exeC-N (= gspD).

machinery_genes.quote: The exeE gene of Aeromonas hydrophila has been shown to be required for the secretion of most if not all of the extracellular proteins produced by this bacterium.
machinery_genes.note: exeE was the founding gene of the exe operon (Jiang & Howard); exeE/exeF isolated prior to the 1993 paper. GspE-family secretion ATPase role from the apparatus brief letter mapping (exeE=gspE).

machinery_genes.quote: The exeE gene and the exeF gene were previously isolated as part of a fragment which complemented this mutant.
machinery_genes.note: exeF named alongside exeE as part of the complementing fragment of the exe operon. GspF-family IM platform role from the apparatus brief letter mapping (exeF=gspF).

machinery_genes.quote: Limited processing of the ExeG protein was observed when it was expressed in Escherichia coli, and this processing was greatly accelerated in the presence of the prepilin peptidase of Pseudomonas aeruginosa.
machinery_genes.note: ExeG is processed by prepilin peptidase, identifying it as the major (pseudo)pilin of the Exe T2SS. One of the five Exe proteins bearing type IV prepilin signal sequences. exeG begins the eight-gene block exeG-exeN sequenced in this paper.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: exeH named only as part of the contiguous exeG-through-exeN block in this paper (no individual functional-characterization quote spelling 'exeH'). Family GspH (minor pseudopilin) assigned from the explicit letter-for-letter exe=gsp/eps mapping; the paper states five Exe proteins carry type IV prepilin signal sequences (the G/H/I/J/K pseudopilins). Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: exeI named only as part of the contiguous exeG-through-exeN block. Family GspI (minor pseudopilin) from the letter mapping; one of the five prepilin-signal-bearing Exe proteins. Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: exeJ named only as part of the contiguous exeG-through-exeN block. Family GspJ (minor pseudopilin) from the letter mapping; one of the five prepilin-signal-bearing Exe proteins. Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: exeK named only as part of the contiguous exeG-through-exeN block. Family GspK (minor pseudopilin) from the letter mapping; one of the five prepilin-signal-bearing Exe proteins. Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds the secretion ATPase)
machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: exeL named only as part of the contiguous exeG-through-exeN block. Family GspL (IM platform, ATPase-binding) from the letter mapping (exeL=gspL=epsL). Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: exeM named only as part of the contiguous exeG-through-exeN block. Family GspM (IM platform) from the letter mapping (exeM=gspM=epsM). Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: exeN is the named 3' terminus of the exe operon block in this paper. Family GspN (accessory) from the letter mapping. Terminal operon gene, lowest-confidence among the pseudopilin/platform block, paralleling the EpsN call in the T2SS_01 pilot.

machinery_genes.role: accessory inner-membrane AAA-ATPase assembly factor (ExeAB complex; secretin assembly)
machinery_genes.quote: Analysis of this fragment revealed an operon-like structure with two complete genes, exeA and exeB ... ExeA indicated that it is a hydrophilic 60-kDa protein with a consensus ATP binding site.
machinery_genes.note: ExeA = GspA. Encoded in the separate exeAB (gspAB) operon, not in exeC-N, but a genuine component of the A. hydrophila Exe T2SS: the ExeAB complex is required to assemble the ExeD secretin (Ast 2002 PMID 11967081; Vanderlinde 2017 PMID 28138102). Included as accessory machinery; outside the labelled exeC-N range. The instance label scopes the operon to exeC-N, so ExeA/ExeB are supplementary, not core to the labelled range.

machinery_genes.role: accessory inner-membrane assembly factor (ExeAB complex; interacts with secretin ExeD)
machinery_genes.quote: ExeB is a 25-kDa basic protein which shares limited homology with PulB, a protein of unknown function associated with the maltose regulon of Klebsiella oxytoca, and OutB, a protein which has been shown to be required for efficient secretion in Erwinia chrysanthemi.
machinery_genes.note: ExeB = GspB. Partner of ExeA in the exeAB operon; ExeB interacts with the ExeD secretin during assembly (Vanderlinde 2017 PMID 28138102). Outside the labelled exeC-N range; included as accessory machinery.

notes: The Exe system is the Aeromonas hydrophila type II secretion system (general secretion pathway / MTB of the GSP), the apparatus that exports aerolysin and other extracellular toxins/enzymes. The original characterization (Howard, Critch & Bedi 1993, J Bacteriol) sequenced exeE/exeF plus the eight-gene block exeG-exeN, the 3' end of the exe operon; the verbatim statement 'a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon' establishes the operon range. Ast et al 2002 explicitly map the nomenclature: 'exeAB (gspAB) and exeC-N (gspC-N)', and ExeD is the outer-membrane secretion-port secretin assembled by the ExeAB complex. Vanderlinde et al 2017 confirms ExeAB + ExeC are required for ExeD secretin assembly and ExeD multimerizes into a dodecameric outer-membrane secretin. The apparatus papers all use A. hydrophila (the archetypal Exe system); A. salmonicida A449 (this instance) carries the orthologous exe operon and is the same T2SS family, so the components transfer. Family assignment uses the letter-for-letter exe=gsp mapping that Ast 2002 states explicitly. Confidence: exeC, exeD, exeE, exeF, exeG carry gene-name-bearing quotes with functional context (high); exeH-exeN are named only within the verbatim contiguous 'exeG through exeN' operon block with families assigned by the explicit gsp letter mapping (medium; exeN terminal/lowest as in the pilot). ExeA and ExeB (GspA/GspB) are the inner-membrane secretin-assembly complex encoded in the separate exeAB operon, included as accessory machinery but flagged as outside the labelled exeC-N range. Deliberately excluded: the prepilin peptidase (GspO/PilD) - the 1993 paper states 'the prepilin peptidase gene does not appear to form part of the exe operon', so it is not part of exeC-N; the secreted substrate aerolysin and the ash3 effector; the Sec/Tat translocon; and the separate sps T2SS that deposits the A. hydrophila S-layer (distinct system). All quotes are verbatim from the cited PubMed abstracts / PMC full texts. All four apparatus DOIs and the review DOI confirmed real via get_article_metadata.
